Assignment metadata:
- objective: IMPLEMENT M15 fГЎze B: Android port.

GOAL: Make MySillyTavern build and run on Android (APK). The app already has Android CI builds in GitHub Actions. FГЎze A (prЕЇzkum) is complete вЂ” the conclusion is GO. The only hard blocker is the `keyring` crate.

### Step 0: Find Android SDK
Run `echo $ANDROID_HOME` and if empty, search for it: `find /home/morthos -maxdepth 4 -name "platform-tools" -type d 2>/dev/null | head -3` or `find /opt -maxdepth 4 -name "sdkmanager" 2>/dev/null`. Also check `~/Android/Sdk`. If NOT found, report: "Android SDK not found вЂ” install it first (https://developer.android.com/studio)" and STOP the remaining steps (only do Step 0).

If SDK IS found, export it: `export ANDROID_HOME=<path>` and continue.

### Step 1: Install Rust Android targets
```bash
rustup target add aarch64-linux-android armv7-linux-androideabi i686-linux-android x86_64-linux-android
```

### Step 2: Replace keyring crate
File: `src-tauri/src/commands/secrets.rs` (or wherever `SecretStore` trait is defined)
- The `keyring` crate uses OS keyring (linux: Secret Service, windows: Credential Manager, mac: Keychain). On Android none of these exist.
- Create a new `AndroidKeyring` implementation of `SecretStore` trait that:
  - On Android: stores API keys in an encrypted shared preferences file (using `tauri-plugin-biometric` or a simple AES-GCM encrypted file in app data dir)
  - On desktop: delegates to existing `keyring` crate
- Use `#[cfg(target_os = "android")]` conditional compilation
- The simplest approach: use a file `api_keys.json` encrypted with a device-derived key (Android Keystore)

File: `src-tauri/Cargo.toml`
- Make `keyring` dependency optional: `keyring = { version = "3", optional = true }`
- Add Android-specific crypto dependency if needed

### Step 3: Conditional compilation for plugins
File: `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` or `src-tauri/src/main.rs`
- `tauri-plugin-dialog` вЂ” on Android, file dialogs don't work natively. Wrap uses with `#[cfg(not(target_os = "android"))]`. For Android, use `intent`-based file picking via a custom Tauri command.
- `tauri-plugin-opener` вЂ” may work via intents on Android, check and wrap if needed
- `tauri-plugin-process` вЂ” `exit`/`relaunch` don't apply on mobile. Wrap with `#[cfg(not(target_os = "android"))]`

File: `src-tauri/Cargo.toml`
- Make `tauri-plugin-dialog`, `tauri-plugin-opener`, `tauri-plugin-process` optional or conditional

### Step 4: Touch/swipe gestures
File: `src/ui/chat/MessageList.tsx` or `src/ui/chat/ChatScreen.tsx`
- Add `onTouchStart`/`onTouchEnd` handlers for swipe left/right on message bubbles
- Swipe left в†’ next variant, swipe right в†’ previous variant
- Track touch start X position, on end compare delta. If > 50px horizontal, trigger variant switch.
- Add `touch-action: pan-y` CSS to prevent default scroll interference

### Step 5: Keyboard/viewport handling
File: `src/ui/chat/ChatInput.tsx`
- On Android, soft keyboard pushes viewport up. Use `visualViewport` API to detect keyboard changes.
- Add `window.visualViewport.addEventListener('resize', ...)` in a useEffect
- Adjust input bar position so it stays above keyboard

### Step 6: Android back button
File: `src/App.tsx` or a new hook
- Listen for Android back button events (Tauri 2 mobile has `onBackPressed` or similar)
- Map back button to: close open panels (Memory, Inventory, Quests) в†’ if none open, navigate back in chat history
- Add to `src/ui/useOnlineStatus.ts` or create `src/ui/useAndroidBack.ts`

### Step 7: Capacities/permissions
File: `src-tauri/capabilities/default.json`
- Ensure Android permissions are declared: INTERNET, ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE
- Add `android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` in generated Android manifest (Tauri should handle this)

### Step 8: Verify
- `cargo check` (with Android target if possible, else desktop)
- `npx tsc --noEmit`
- `npm test`

CONSTRAINTS:
- All desktop functionality must continue working unchanged
- Android-specific code behind `#[cfg(target_os = "android")]` or `typeof window !== 'undefined'` checks
- Keyring replacement must be transparent вЂ” the `SecretStore` trait stays the same
- NO changes to core business logic вЂ” only platform adaptation

OUTPUT: Summary of all files changed and verification results. If Android SDK not found, report that clearly and skip Step 1-8.

[package]
name = "mysillytavern"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "A Tauri App"
authors = ["you"]
edition = "2021"

# See more keys and their definitions at https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/manifest.html

[lib]
# The `_lib` suffix may seem redundant but it is necessary
# to make the lib name unique and wouldn't conflict with the bin name.
# This seems to be only an issue on Windows, see https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/issues/8519
name = "mysillytavern_lib"
crate-type = ["staticlib", "cdylib", "rlib"]

[build-dependencies]
tauri-build = { version = "2", features = [] }

[dependencies]
tauri = { version = "2", features = ["protocol-asset"] }
tauri-plugin-opener = "2"

tauri-plugin-dialog = "2"

  ]
}

name: Build Android

on:
  push:
    branches: [master]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Setup Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: aarch64-linux-android,armv7-linux-androideabi,i686-linux-android,x86_64-linux-android

      - name: Setup Android SDK
        uses: android-actions/setup-android@v3
        with:
          packages: "platforms;android-34 build-tools;34.0.0 ndk;27.0.12077973"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Install cargo-ndk
        run: cargo install cargo-ndk

      - name: Init Android project
        run: npx tauri android init
      - name: Build Tauri Android (APK + AAB)
        run: npm run tauri android build
        env:
          ANDROID_HOME: ${{ steps.setup-android.outputs.ANDROID_HOME }}
          NDK_HOME: ${{ steps.setup-android.outputs.ANDROID_NDK }}

      - name: Upload Android artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: MySillyTavern-Android
          path: |
            src-tauri/gen/android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/*.apk
            src-tauri/gen/android/app/build/outputs/bundle/release/*.aab

content.thinking:
I notice a bug in `handleTouchEnd`: 

```js
const deltaX = e.changedTouches[0]?.clientX ?? 0 - touchStartXRef.current;
```

Operator precedence: `??` has lower precedence than `-`, so this is parsed as:
```
e.changedTouches[0]?.clientX ?? (0 - touchStartXRef.current)
```

If `e.changedTouches[0]?.clientX` is undefined, it evaluates to `0 - touchStartXRef.current` which is negative. That's wrong.

The correct expression should be:
```
(e.changedTouches[0]?.clientX ?? 0) - touchStartXRef.current
```

Let me fix this.

### SUMMARY
Implemented M15 Phase B Android port adaptations across 17 files (2 new, 15 modified). The alleged hard blocker (`keyring` crate) was already resolved вЂ” `secrets.rs` uses a `FileStore` with JSON files, no `keyring` dependency exists. Made `tauri-plugin-dialog` and `tauri-plugin-process` optional behind a `desktop-plugins` Cargo feature (excluded on Android via `--no-default-features`). Created `src/platform.ts` with safe wrappers for dialog/process/opener that catch import failures on Android. Added touch-swipe gesture support for message variant switching, `visualViewport`-based soft keyboard handling, Android back-button panel-close logic, and `touch-action: pan-y` CSS. Updated 9 files that directly imported `@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog` to use the safe wrappers. Shell execution was unavailable in this sub-agent, so Steps 0 (SDK detection), 1 (target installation), and 8 (verification) could not be performed.
